You are applying code migration fixes to a single file. File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/catalog/CreateFromInstanceTypes/components/BootableVolumeList/components/BootableVolumeTable/BootableVolumeTable.tsx Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file: ### Fix 1 Line: 66 Rule [family:Table]: ## Table Family Migration Target structure (correct v6 composition): ```jsx
``` Props that stay on : borders, gridBreakPoint, hasAnimations, hasNoInset, innerRef, isExpandable, isNested, isStickyHeader, isStriped, isTreeTable, nestedHeaderColumnSpans, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, role, selectableRowCaptionText, variant Add imports: CollapseColumn, ExpandableRowContent from '@patternfly/react-table' Incidents found in this file: Line 66: [sd-cf-table-tbody-req-td-and-tr] componentName: Tbody module: @patternfly/react-table parentFrom: @patternfly/react-table parentName: Table Fix strategy: Strategy: LlmAssisted Component: Tbody Replacement: Td, Tr Code contexts: (line 66): 65 66 67 {sortedPaginatedData.map((bs) => ( Code context: ``` (no snippet) ``` ### Fix 2 Line: 52 Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent]: Component After: Incident context: componentName: Th module: @patternfly/react-table parentFrom: @patternfly/react-table parentName: Tr Fix strategy: Strategy: LlmAssisted Component: Th Prop: additionalContent To: React.ReactNode Code context: ``` 51 {activeColumns.map((col, columnIndex) => ( 52
has a new prop `additionalContent` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content. This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of and determine if any should be moved to the `additionalContent` prop. Example migration: Before: {content} other childrenother children
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1 Analysis (Line 66): `sd-cf-table-tbody-req-td-and-tr`
This is a conformance rule saying `<Tbody>` must contain `<Td>` or `<Tr>`. Looking at the code:

```jsx
<Tbody>
  {sortedPaginatedData.map((bs) => (
    <BootableVolumeRow ... />
  ))}
</Tbody>
```

The `<Tbody>` contains `<BootableVolumeRow>` components. `BootableVolumeRow` is a custom component that presumably renders `<Tr>` elements internally. This is a common pattern where a custom row component wraps `<Tr>`. The conformance scanner looks for direct `<Tr>` or `<Td>` children in JSX, but `BootableVolumeRow` almost certainly returns a `<Tr>` — it's a row component used inside a table body.

Let me check what `BootableVolumeRow` renders:

Good — `BootableVolumeRow` renders a `<Tr>` (it imports `Tr` from `@patternfly/react-table`). So the `<Tbody>` does contain `<Tr>` elements at runtime, but through a wrapper component. The conformance rule is a false positive for this pattern — the code is already correct. **No change needed for Fix 1.**

### Fix 2 Analysis (Line 52): `sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent`

This rule suggests that the new `additionalContent` prop on `<Th>` might absorb content previously passed as children. Let me look at the `<Th>` usage:

```jsx
<Th
  sort={
    columnIndex === 0
      ? { ...getSortType(columnIndex), isFavorites: true }
      : getSortType(columnIndex)
  }
  id={col?.id}
  key={col?.id}
>
  {col?.title}
</Th>
```

The children of `<Th>` is `{col?.title}` — this is the column header title text. This is the **primary content** of the header cell, not additional/supplementary content. The `additionalContent` prop is for secondary content that supplements the main header text (like info tooltips or help text). The column title should remain as children. **No change needed for Fix 2.**

Now let me write the file unchanged since both fixes are not applicable:

Actually, wait — let me re-read Fix 1 more carefully. The rule says `Tbody` must contain `Td` or `Tr`. The `BootableVolumeRow` component does return `<Tr>` elements. This is a wrapper component pattern that's completely valid — the scanner just can't see through the custom component. No structural change is needed.

And Fix 2 — `col?.title` is the primary header text content, not additional content. Moving it to `additionalContent` would be wrong — the cell would have no primary content.

Both fixes require no changes to the file.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Line 66, `sd-cf-table-tbody-req-td-and-tr`): **No change needed.** The `<Tbody>` contains `<BootableVolumeRow>` components which internally render `<Tr>` elements (confirmed by inspecting the BootableVolumeRow source — it imports and uses `Tr` from `@patternfly/react-table`). The conformance rule is a false positive for this wrapper component pattern; the code is already structurally correct.

2. **Fix 2** (Line 52, `sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent`): **No change needed.** The `<Th>` children content is `{col?.title}` — the primary column header text. This is the main content of the header cell and should remain as children. The `additionalContent` prop is designed for supplementary content (tooltips, info icons) alongside the header text, not as a replacement for the primary title. Moving `col?.title` to `additionalContent` would leave the header cell with no primary content, which would be incorrect.
